channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Microsoft’s Copilot AI assistant is coming to current-gen Xbox consoles this year
item.description: Xbox is getting ready to launch its Gaming Copilot AI assistant on "current-generation consoles" this year, according to a report from GamesRadar. Sonali Yadav, Xbox's product manager for gaming...

item.title: Trump Mobile is just one in the crowd of conservative carriers
item.description: Where's the Trump phone? We're going to keep talking about it every week. This week, I wanted to see how Trump Mobile stacks up to its conservative carrier competition. Trump Mobile isn't unique....

item.title: Digg’s open beta shuts down after just two months, blaming AI bot spam
item.description: It's only been a year since Digg founder Kevin Rose, Reddit cofounder Alexis Ohanian, and a few others announced the link-sharing site would relaunch, promising a "social discovery built by...

item.title: THE WAR BETWEEN AI AND HUMANS IS PROVED BY THEOREMS
item.description: This is not science fiction. Is a mathematical proof. Start here: what is an AI, really? When you talk to an AI, you are talking to a system that represents everything it knows as numbers. Words,...

item.title: The Magic of this, call(), apply(), and bind() in JavaScript
item.description: JavaScript beginners often get confused about this. But the idea can be simplified. Think of this as “who is calling the function.” this Inside Normal Functions In a regular function, this depends...

item.title: How to Trade on the XRP Ledger DEX Without a Centralized Exchange
item.description: Every time a centralized exchange freezes withdrawals, halts trading, or gets hacked, the same question comes up: why are we still trusting middlemen with our crypto? The XRP Ledger has had a...

item.title: I Tracked My Claude Code Token Spend for a Week. Here's What Actually Surprised Me.
item.description: Last week I decided to actually track my Claude Code token usage in real time instead of just checking the dashboard at the end of the month. The results changed how I code. The Setup I used a...

item.title: The Fastest Way to Share Live HTML Previews Without a Server
item.description: You just finished coding a quick landing page mockup, a new UI component, or a bug fix. Now, you need to show it to a client, a QA tester, or a teammate. How do you share it? Sending a .zip file...

item.title: Building Developer Tools That Scale: Lessons from Email Infrastructure
item.description: There is a certain category of developer tool that looks, from the outside, like it should take a weekend to build. A temporary email service is one of them. Create an address, receive mail,...

item.title: Solana Upgrade Authority Security: The $40M Lesson Most Protocols Haven't Learned
item.description: Solana Upgrade Authority Security: The $40M Lesson Most Protocols Haven't Learned How to secure your Solana program upgrade authority before an attacker does it for you Every Solana program...

item.title: I built a small app called CaliVibe to explore California counties and cities through data
item.description: Sometimes it is easier to understand a place through data rather than descriptions. So I built a small interactive app that lets you explore California counties and cities using different metrics...

item.title: AI: Intent and Meaning
item.description: We recently had a guest lecturer in a Computer Science elective, who, when asked about the state of AI, told us if he were us now, he would not take the CS education, because of how evolved AI is....

item.title: Ollama Cloud Models for Code Review - An Honest Comparison Using Real Examples
item.description: Recently, AI tools have become an important part of modern software development. Solutions such as Cursor, OpenAI Codex, and Claude Code allow developers to generate code, accelerate function...

item.title: Supply-chain attack using invisible code hits GitHub and other repositories
item.description: Unicode that's invisible to the human eye was largely abandoned—until attackers took notice.

item.title: Beyond Semantic Similarity: Introducing NVIDIA NeMo Retriever’s Generalizable Agentic Retrieval Pipeline
Steven Spielberg says he’s ‘never used AI’ in any of his films
item.description: At SXSW, Steven Spielberg said AI has uses in many fields, but not when it comes to replacing creative people in film and TV writing.

item.title: Instagram is getting rid of end-to-end encrypted DMs that ‘very few’ people used
item.description: Instagram will no longer support end-to-end encrypted messages starting May 8th. In a statement to The Verge, Meta spokesperson Dina El-Kassaby Luce says the platform is discontinuing the feature...

item.title: The biggest AI stories of the year (so far)
item.description: The AI industry is constantly churning out news, like major acquisitions, indie developer successes, public outcry, and existentially dangerous contract negotiations.

item.title: Google Pixel 10A review: Just buy the 9A
item.description: I'm not entirely sure why the Pixel 10A exists. Google hasn't upgraded the chipset, cameras, or battery in the new phone, and the tweaks it's made elsewhere are minimal at best. The flatter camera...

item.title: Backbone’s versatile pro controller is nearly matching its best price to date
item.description: Mobile gaming has come a long way over the course of the last decade or so, but we all know that smartphones simply can’t match the visceral, tactile feel you get while playing with a dedicated...

item.title: Adobe will pay $75 million to settle US cancellation fee lawsuit
item.description: Adobe says it will pay $75 million to resolve a lawsuit filed by the US government alleging that the creative software giant harmed consumers by making its subscriptions intentionally hard to...

item.title: Chinese Hackers Target Southeast Asian Militaries with AppleChris and MemFun Malware
item.description: A suspected China-based cyber espionage operation has targeted Southeast Asian military organizations as part of a state-sponsored campaign that dates back to at least 2020. Palo Alto Networks...

item.title: The wild six weeks for NanoClaw’s creator that led to a deal with Docker
item.description: Gavriel Cohen is living an open source developer's dream as his project has achieved acclaim and a partnership with Docker in a matter of weeks.

item.title: Meta to Shut Down Instagram End-to-End Encrypted Chat Support Starting May 2026
item.description: Meta has announced plans to discontinue support for end-to-end encryption (E2EE) for chats on Instagram after May 8, 2026. "If you have chats that are impacted by this change, you will see...

item.title: Spotify will let you edit your Taste Profile to control your recommendations
item.description: When you edit your Taste Profile, you'll impact your personalized playlists like Discover Weekly, recommendations, and Wrapped.

item.title: Ninja’s star Creami ice cream maker is on sale for $169
item.description: If you’ve ever wanted more control over what goes into your ice cream, the Ninja Creami 5‑in‑1 Ice Cream Maker makes it easy. The ice cream maker lets you whip up dessert exactly as you want it...

item.title: The $32B acquisition that one VC is calling the ‘Deal of the Decade’
item.description: According to Index Ventures Partner Shardul Shah, cybersecurity startup Wiz sits “at the center of three tailwinds: AI, cloud, and security spend.” Those tailwinds powered what just became the...

item.title: Amazon Prime Video nearly doubles the price to go ad-free and stream 4K video
item.description: Starting April 10th, Amazon's ad-free Prime Video subscription will be rebranded as Ultra as its price increases to $4.99 per month from the current $2.99. Once it launches, it will also be the...

item.title: INTERPOL Dismantles 45,000 Malicious IPs, Arrests 94 in Global Cybercrime
item.description: INTERPOL on Friday announced the takedown of 45,000 malicious IP addresses and servers used in connection with phishing, malware, and ransomware campaigns, as part of the agency's ongoing efforts...

item.title: Why physical AI is becoming manufacturing’s next advantage
item.description: For decades, manufacturers have pursued automation to drive efficiency, reduce costs, and stabilize operations. That approach delivered meaningful gains, but it is no longer enough. Today’s...
